navigation.title: Product overview
navigation.description: What Seonic inspects today, how it works, and what it is trying to make faster.

metadata.title: Product Overview
metadata.description: Understand Seonic's current inspection model, dashboard surfaces, and validation workflow.

hero.title: A local workspace for Next.js SEO inspection
hero.lead: Seonic is built for the moment when a team needs to understand route metadata, preview the result, and decide what to fix without leaving the development environment.

sections.title: What problem Seonic is solving
sections.lead: SEO metadata in Next.js usually gets split across route files, configuration, generated output, and conventions that are easy to forget.

blocks.content: That creates a practical problem for engineering teams: it is hard to answer what this route will expose, whether it is good enough, and where to change it without checking multiple files and tools.

items: Metadata can live in metadata, generateMetadata, generated config, or route-local files.
Missing canonical URLs, descriptions, OG images, and structured data tend to surface late.
Duplicate titles and descriptions are easy to miss when routes are reviewed one file at a time.
Teams need previews and generated artifacts, not just a lint result.

sections.title: Core surfaces
sections.lead: The current product surface is already organized around the questions developers ask first when they inspect an app.

items.title: Dashboard overview
items.body: See total routes, average score, routes with issues, warning counts, and coverage across configured, detected, and unconfigured routes.

items.title: Route detail
items.body: Open a single route to review previews, metadata fields, hreflang, JSON-LD, warnings, score, and guidance for creating a matching .seonic.ts file.

items.title: Map and filters
items.body: Filter the route map by configured, detected, unconfigured, or issue-heavy paths so you can work on the routes that need attention first.

items.title: Generated outputs
items.body: Preview or generate sitemap and feed output from the same resolved route data instead of rebuilding those artifacts separately.

sections.title: How the inspection model works
sections.lead: Seonic combines route scanning, metadata detection, optional live fetching, and validation into one local loop.

items.title: Scan the route tree
items.body: The CLI reads app/ or src/app/, resolves route paths, and records dynamic segments.

items.title: Detect existing metadata
items.body: Static metadata exports and generateMetadata are detected from route source so Seonic can distinguish existing metadata from routes that still need configuration.

items.title: Merge Seonic config
items.body: Global defaults from seonic.config.* and route-level .seonic.ts files are merged into a single route view.

items.title: Optionally fetch live output
items.body: When devServer.url is configured, Seonic can fetch rendered metadata from the running Next.js server and override static guesses with live output.

sections.title: What Seonic validates today
sections.lead: The current warning set is intentionally opinionated toward issues that create bad previews, weak snippets, or inconsistent indexing behavior.

items: Missing or weak title and description values, including length checks.
Missing Open Graph title, description, or image.
Missing hreflang tags when i18n is configured.
Duplicate titles or descriptions across routes.
Conflicts such as a route excluded from sitemap while still marked indexable.

sections.title: What is already real and what is next
sections.lead: The roadmap is easiest to understand when separated into present capabilities and the next layer of precision.

items.title: Already in place
items.body: Route scanning, metadata detection, local dashboard pages, score and warning summaries, sitemap and feed generation, and .seonic.ts file generation.

items.title: Next layer
items.body: Stronger source trace, deeper metadata extraction, better live route coverage, and a tighter path from warning to the exact code that should change.

items.title: Longer term
items.body: A broader adapter model for frameworks beyond Next.js once the core Next.js inspection workflow becomes stable.

cta.title: Need the provenance story?
cta.lead: The source trace page explains what Seonic can identify today, what is still route-level rather than field-level, and why that distinction matters.
